$comment: SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT. feedpak rigs.json (engine-agnostic rig library). See spec/feedpak-v1.md §7.9.
title: feedpak rigs

id.$comment: Stable id referenced by arrangement tones.base_rig / changes[].rig.

channels.$comment: Channel count the rig is authored for; default 1 (mono).

blocks.$comment: Ordered serial signal chain (input → block[0] → … → output) unless `graph` overrides.

ext.$comment: Vendor/experimental namespace; readers MUST preserve verbatim.

block.$comment: One effect stage. `intent` says what it is (engine-agnostic); `realizations` say how to render it.

id.$comment: Required only if the block is referenced from `graph`.

role.$comment: Open vocabulary: gain, drive, amp, cab, eq, dynamics, modulation, delay, reverb, pitch, filter, utility, … Unknown values MUST be preserved.

realizations.$comment: Ordered by preference; a reader renders the FIRST realization whose `engine` it supports. If it supports none, it falls back to `intent`.

intent.$comment: Engine-independent description of the block, so a reader with no matching realization can still display/approximate/map it.

kind.$comment: Open vocabulary: amp, cab, drive, fuzz, compressor, eq, chorus, phaser, flanger, tremolo, delay, reverb, pitch, gate, boost, …

model.$comment: Non-normative model hint. MUST NOT be a trademarked product name in distributed packs.

realization.$comment: A concrete way to render the block. New engines are new `engine` values; older readers skip what they don't support.

engine.$comment: Open vocabulary. Reserved values: 'nam' (Neural Amp Modeler capture), 'ir' (impulse response), 'plugin' (host plugin), 'builtin' (host DSP by id).

ref.$comment: For 'nam'/'ir': pack-relative path (no leading '/', no '..') or absolute URI to the asset.

sha256.$comment: Optional integrity hash of the referenced asset.

id.$comment: For 'plugin': unique/bundle id; for 'builtin': the host DSP id.

state.$comment: Optional opaque plugin state, base64. Exact-recall; never the ONLY representation (pair with intent/params).

params.$comment: Open map of named controls. A value is either a normalized number in 0..1, or a typed object {value, unit}. Discrete/enum controls use a string. Not limited to any fixed knob set.

automationLane.$comment: Time-varying value for one param. Points are time-sorted; value held until the next point.

graph.$comment: Optional explicit topology for non-serial rigs (parallel amps, wet/dry, stereo split/merge). Node ids are block ids plus the reserved 'input' and 'output'. When omitted, `blocks` is a serial chain.
